picks.narrative: The model prices KC at 53.2% to win where the no-vig market implies 41.7%, a 11.7pp disagreement. Several smaller signals stack the same way — pitcher quality (xfip) (~+1.0pp), defense (oaa) (~+0.6pp) — with no single one dominating. The main pushback is home field (~-0.7pp): MET's home-field context weighs against the pick playing on the road. The market has drifted against the pick since posting, widening the available price — the current +141 still sits inside the playable range (to +103). As a cluster edge rather than one dominant mismatch, it is a thinner, more fragile signal — worth sizing accordingly.

picks.narrative: IBP makes COL 42.3% fair versus a 32.0% no-vig market price — a 10.8pp gap. The largest lift is pitcher quality (xfip) (~+1.2pp): Gabriel Hughes 4.20 xFIP vs Roki Sasaki 5.54 xFIP. The main pushback is home field (~-1.1pp): LAD's home-field context weighs against the pick playing on the road. With a headwind that size, this is not a clean all-signal read. The market has drifted against the pick since posting, widening the available price — the current +217 still sits inside the playable range (to +161). The path is exploiting the LAD weakness the model flags before they can stabilize.

picks.narrative: Model and market disagree on PIT by 7.2pp — 59.2% fair against a 52.0% price. The largest lift is pitcher quality (xfip) (~+1.2pp): Jared Jones 3.69 xFIP vs Grant Holmes 4.97 xFIP. The main pushback is defense (oaa) (~-0.6pp): ATL defense grades +26 OAA better on the season — a run-prevention headwind. The market has drifted against the pick since posting, widening the available price — the current -108 still sits inside the playable range (to -123). The win condition is five-plus quality innings from Jared Jones and avoiding early bullpen exposure.

picks.narrative: A 6.8pp gap: IBP has MIN at 60.8%, the market at 53.7%. The model isn't leaning on a marquee matchup here — no single baseball factor rises to a headline; the disagreement is with the posted price itself. The main pushback is defense (oaa) (~-0.6pp): The model's defensive-efficiency adjustment leans to the opponent. The price has held roughly steady since posting — the current -117 still sits inside the playable range (to -131). This isn't a clean all-signal agreement — it's the model calling the posted number too generous for the true win probability.

picks.narrative: IBP values MIA 6.6pp higher than the market — 52.7% model vs 45.7% implied. Several smaller signals stack the same way — defense (oaa) (~+0.6pp), starter rest (~+0.6pp) — with no single one dominating. The main pushback is pitcher quality (xfip) (~-0.9pp): Tyler Phillips 4.65 xFIP vs George Kirby 3.65 xFIP. The price has held roughly steady since posting — the current +117 still sits inside the playable range (to +106). The case is a stack of small same-direction signals, not one dominant mismatch — treat it as the thinner edge it is.

picks.narrative: The model sees STL as a 48.0% side; the market is pricing 42.6%, leaving a 5.4pp edge. Several smaller signals stack the same way — lineup quality (~+0.9pp), defense (oaa) (~+0.6pp), home field (~+0.5pp) — with no single one dominating. The main pushback is pitcher quality (xfip) (~-1.4pp): Michael McGreevy 4.60 xFIP vs Kyle Harrison 3.10 xFIP. With a headwind that size, this is not a clean all-signal read. The price has held roughly steady since posting — the current +135 still sits inside the playable range (to +127). The case is a stack of small same-direction signals, not one dominant mismatch — treat it as the thinner edge it is.
